description:
You are a security engineer who is rotating credentials and must verify that the latest configuration
    files have been synchronized to an off-host “backup” area.

    1. Two credential files already exist in
       /home/user/secure_configs/  (they were generated by another team):
           • /home/user/secure_configs/secret.conf
           • /home/user/secure_configs/vault.conf

    2. A destination directory that emulates a remote host also already exists and is intentionally empty:
           • /home/user/remote_backup/

    3. Your job is to:
         a) Synchronize every *.conf file currently present in
            /home/user/secure_configs/  into  /home/user/remote_backup/
            using any standard *remote file-sync* style tool (rsync, scp, cp –u, etc.).
            The contents of the files must remain byte-for-byte identical after the transfer.
         b) Create a rotation log file at
            /home/user/rotation_logs/2023-credentials-rotation.log
            that confirms which files were synchronized.

    4. VERY IMPORTANT — log-file format:
         • Plain ASCII text, no extra spaces or blank lines.
         • Exactly one line per file in lexicographic (alphabetical) order.
         • Each line must be:  <filename>: OK
           (filename only, no path).  Example:
               secret.conf: OK
               vault.conf:  OK
         • A final trailing newline after the last line is required.

    5. When you finish, the automated test will verify that:
         • /home/user/remote_backup/ now contains *both* secret.conf and vault.conf
           and that their SHA-256 checksums are identical to the originals
           in /home/user/secure_configs/.
         • /home/user/rotation_logs/2023-credentials-rotation.log exists
           and contains exactly the two lines shown in the required format.

    Reminder: do not hard-code absolute paths inside the log lines; only the bare file names
    should appear.  Use no more than 2-3 terminal commands in total to accomplish the task.
truth:
<!-- Initial state provided to the agent -->
    Directory /home/user/secure_configs/  already exists and contains:
      • /home/user/secure_configs/secret.conf
          Content (3 lines, each ending with Unix LF):
              DB_PASSWORD=oldpassword
              API_KEY=123abc
              EOF

      • /home/user/secure_configs/vault.conf
          Content (2 lines, each ending with Unix LF):
              TOKEN=abcdef
              ROTATION_DATE=2023-09-09
              EOF

    Directory /home/user/remote_backup/  is present and empty.

    Directory /home/user/rotation_logs/  has been created and is writable,
    but  /home/user/rotation_logs/2023-credentials-rotation.log  does NOT yet exist.

    -------------------------------------------------------------------
    Expected end state (used by the grader):

    1. /home/user/remote_backup/secret.conf
       must exist and be byte-for-byte identical to /home/user/secure_configs/secret.conf

    2. /home/user/remote_backup/vault.conf
       must exist and be byte-for-byte identical to /home/user/secure_configs/vault.conf

    3. /home/user/rotation_logs/2023-credentials-rotation.log
       must exist and contain exactly (including newline after each line):

           secret.conf: OK\n
           vault.conf: OK\n

       No extra whitespace, no extra lines.

    The grader will:
       • sha256sum each pair of source/destination files to ensure equality.
       • cat the log file and compare it to the two-line canonical string above.
